title: Malaxis warapussae

The genus Malaxis is a group of orchids commonly known as the "baldypens" or "baldypens orchids." The specific classification of Malaxis warapussae is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other orchid species.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties.
